 {"id":"beads_rust-oxmd","title":"EPIC: E2E Tests for Untested CLI Commands","description":"# EPIC: E2E Tests for Untested CLI Commands

## Current State (Updated)
Most commands now have comprehensive E2E tests:

### Fully Covered (191+ E2E tests)
- basic_lifecycle: 18 tests (create, update, delete, close, reopen cycle)
- audit: 18 tests
- changelog: 15 tests
- comments: 13 tests
- errors: 22 tests
- graph: 9 tests
- labels: 16 tests
- queries: 6 tests
- ready: 20 tests
- relations: 6 tests
- sync (5 files): 49+ tests

### Still Need E2E Tests (8 commands)
1. **history command** (beads_rust-axr4) - 11+ tests planned
2. **orphans command** (beads_rust-mgpi) - 9+ tests planned
3. **epic command** (beads_rust-g3xk) - 12+ tests planned
4. **upgrade command** (beads_rust-21kv) - 7+ tests planned
5. **completions command** (beads_rust-5ui7) - 10+ tests planned
6. **q (quick capture)** (beads_rust-3gbd) - 15+ tests planned [NEW]
7. **lint command** (beads_rust-390j) - 15+ tests planned [NEW]
8. **defer/undefer** (beads_rust-fmxd) - 22+ tests planned [NEW]

## Success Criteria
- All 8 remaining commands have dedicated E2E test files
- Expected test count: 100+ new tests
- All test files include comprehensive logging

## Standard Test File Structure
Each test file should follow this pattern:
\`\`\`
tests/e2e_<command>.rs
├── mod success_tests - Happy path tests
├── mod error_tests - Error handling tests
├── mod edge_case_tests - Boundary conditions
└── mod json_output_tests - JSON format verification
\`\`\`

## Logging Requirements for All Tests
- Log test entry with timestamp
- Log all command executions with args
- Log stdout/stderr captured
- Log assertions being made
- Log test exit with duration","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"CopperLake","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:24:50.667587884Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:09:56.306343118Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:09:56.306343118Z","close_reason":"All child E2E tasks complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-an3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-fmxd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-oz14","title":"[MEDIUM] comment ordering is unstable for same-second timestamps","description":"Root cause: src/storage/sqlite.rs:4967,4997,5698 order comments only by created_at, but add_comment stores CURRENT_TIMESTAMP with second precision; multiple comments in the same second can be returned/exported in storage-engine order instead of insertion order. Repro: insert two comments with the same created_at and descending ids, then get_comments/get_all_comments can emit the higher id first depending on the query plan. Proposed fix: add id ASC as a tie-breaker for per-issue, batch, and export comment queries. Test to add: storage_crud regression with same timestamp comments preserving id order.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:31:31.819135958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:44.498610621Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:44.498341498Z","close_reason":"Fixed deterministic same-timestamp comment ordering by adding id tie-breakers; added storage regression coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":300,"issue_id":"beads_rust-oz14","author":"cod_1","text":"degraded-coordination: files: src/storage/sqlite.rs, tests/storage_crud.rs","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:31:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-p0jx","title":"Fix clippy/fmt failures for -D warnings","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T23:52:31.406109363Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T23:55:43.900445142Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T23:55:43.900445142Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of second-9wm","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
+{"id":"beads_rust-p7xo","title":"Dead code behind #[allow(dead_code)]: 3 functions/structs with zero callers","description":"/mock-code-finder scan found 3 genuine dead-code items annotated with #[allow(dead_code)] that have full implementations but zero callers anywhere in src/:

1. **src/cli/commands/audit.rs:443** — `no_fields_provided(args: &AuditRecordArgs) -> bool`
   Checks whether all audit record fields are empty/None. Was likely intended as a validation guard before recording, but `execute_audit_record()` never calls it. The sibling `is_empty_opt()` IS used (by `clean_opt`).
   Remediation: wire into audit record validation or delete.

2. **src/storage/sqlite.rs:5297** — `collect_descendant_ids(&self, parent_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>>`
   Full BFS traversal over parent_id foreign keys. Implemented but never called — the codebase currently has no need to walk the subtree. Only one hit in grep.
   Remediation: delete; re-implement if subtree queries are needed later.

3. **src/sync/mod.rs:7914+7933** — `progress_bar()` and `progress_spinner()`
   Module-private helpers that duplicate `util::progress::{create_progress_bar, create_spinner}`, which are the functions actually imported and used (sync/mod.rs:24, lines 1733/3328/3589).
   Remediation: delete both; util::progress is the canonical source.

Two other `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations were verified as FALSE POSITIVES:
- `close.rs:103 CloseExecution` — all 4 fields are used (lines 384-409)
- `sync.rs:74 SyncPathPolicy` — struct has 9 references; annotation covers specific fields

None of these dead items affect correctness. This is pure cleanup debt.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"debt","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:52:13.564987925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:52:13.564987925Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["arch","debt","mock-code-finder"]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-p8kw","title":"Merge --force-jsonl can resurrect local tombstones","description":"Round 2 review finding: execute_merge calls three_way_merge with tombstones=None, and three_way_merge's tombstone guard only skips resurrection when the tombstoned ID is absent from local. If the local DB contains a tombstone and JSONL contains a non-tombstone record for the same ID, --force-jsonl can choose the JSONL winner and resurrect a deleted issue. Import paths block this, so merge should preserve tombstone protection too.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:21:28.395652971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:30:31.579625719Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:30:31.579332100Z","close_reason":"Merge now passes local tombstone IDs into the three-way merge engine and blocks non-tombstone JSONL winners from resurrecting them; unit and e2e regressions cover force-jsonl.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-pfx","title":"Conformance Test Harness","description":"# Conformance Test Harness

## Current State: IMPLEMENTED
The test harness is fully implemented in tests/conformance.rs:

### Infrastructure Complete
- ConformanceWorkspace struct with paired br/bd directories
- Logging to files under logs/ directory
- Multiple CompareMode variants:
  - ExactJson
  - NormalizedJson (handles timestamps, IDs)
  - ContainsFields
  - ExitCodeOnly
  - ArrayUnordered
  - FieldsExcluded
  - StructureOnly

### Utilities Complete
- normalize_json() for timestamp/ID normalization
- diff_json() for human-readable diff output
- extract_json_payload() for JSON extraction

### Benchmark Infrastructure Complete
- BenchmarkConfig (warmup_runs, timed_runs, outlier_threshold)
- TimingStats (mean, median, p95, std_dev)
- run_benchmark() with outlier filtering

### Test Scenarios Framework
- TestScenario struct for reusable scenarios
- Pre-defined scenarios in scenarios module

## Remaining Work
- None - harness is feature complete
- Consider closing this bead","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"CyanBeaver","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T06:35:08.494717844Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:24.790022903Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:24.790022903Z","close_reason":"Conformance Test Harness is fully implemented in tests/conformance.rs with all required features: ConformanceWorkspace, multiple CompareMode variants, JSON normalization, diff utilities, benchmark infrastructure, and test scenarios framework.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-pg7c","title":"Benchmark suite: synthetic scale-up (100k+ issues)","description":"Add a synthetic scale-up path to stress br/bd beyond existing datasets.

Scope
- Generate large issue sets by cloning/expanding real datasets or via br CLI to preserve realistic distributions.
- Exercise list/search/ready/sync on 100k+ issues and dense dependency graphs.
- Capture time + RSS + export/import sizes.

Acceptance
- Opt-in only (env flag) and clearly marked as long-running.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Opus-45","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:42:12.340456247Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T08:45:53.504603494Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T08:45:53.504603494Z","close_reason":"Implementation verified: tests/bench_synthetic_scale.rs contains complete synthetic scale-up benchmark suite with (1) Scale tiers: Small 10k, Medium 50k, Large 100k, XLarge 250k issues, (2) Synthetic dataset generation with realistic issue title patterns and configurable dependency density, (3) Operations benchmarked: list, list_open, ready, stats, search, blocked, export, (4) Metrics: duration, peak RSS, output size, issues/second throughput, (5) Env-gated stress tests (BR_E2E_STRESS=1), (6) JSON output with detailed metrics and summary. All unit tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-pg7c","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ag35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-pg7c","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-owu6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":164,"issue_id":"beads_rust-pg7c","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Synthetic scale-up should preserve realistic distributions (priority/status/type) by cloning from real datasets, not random mocks. Mark all stress tests as opt-in via env flag (e.g., BR_E2E_STRESS=1).","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:43:55Z"}]}
